assumptions: User stories docs (AGENT_LUDUM_USER_STORIES.md, HOARD_HURT_HELP_USER_STORIES.md) do not exist yet — design decisions are sourced from AGENT_LUDUM_DESIGN.md §6 and HOARD_HURT_HELP_DESIGN.md §5
Game admin routes live at app/routes/game_admin_web.py and app/routes/game_admin_api.py (parameterised by game slug) rather than per-game directories — to be confirmed in plan
non_goals: Any change to match management logic, turn loop, scheduling, export logic, or bot seeding behaviour
Any change to the GameModule contract
Changes to public spectator UX or player-facing pages
Role-based UI element hiding beyond what auth guards already enforce
acceptance_criteria: Game admin for HHH cannot reach another game's admin routes (returns 403)
Spectator API reveals strategy prompts only to game admins for that specific game, not to any admin
New boundary tests cover cross-game access denial
PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS and per-game GAME_ADMIN_EMAILS config replaces single admin emails setting

checklist.goal: Split the single 'require_admin' auth boundary into two distinct roles: Platform Admin (manages game catalog, admin allowlist, platform health) and Game Admin (manages matches of a specific game — scoped, no cross-game access). The same person can hold both; the code enforces the boundary structurally regardless.
checklist.audience: Internal admins — Chris initially holds both roles; future ops teams where platform duties and game-specific match management may split between people.
checklist.constraints: Must not change match management logic, turn loop, scheduling, export logic, or bot seeding. GameModule contract unchanged. CLAUDE.md standards apply: no type:ignore, full async, typed signatures. Both roles use Google email allowlists.
